      "new_str": "// ----- UI: animation mode dropdown --------------------------------------\nconst animationSelect = document.getElementById('animation-mode');\nanimationSelect.addEventListener('change', () => {\n  // Discard every preset's in-flight state. Switching modes mid-stroke\n  // shouldn't leave half-fallen rain or a curving sun-sweep continuing\n  // into the new scene.\n  clearAllAnimationState();\n  animationMode = animationSelect.value;\n});\n\n// ============================================================\n// TIME OF DAY \u2014 paper color animation layer (v0.12)\n// ============================================================\n// Independent from the weather animation: each time preset has a target\n// paper color (PAPER_R/G/B_BASE), and selecting one starts a smooth LERP\n// from the current substrate to that target. The LERP runs for\n// TIME_LERP_FRAMES, applying theme + body-bg every frame and forcing a\n// full render so the canvas reflects the new color in real time.\n//\n// Targets are visual moods, not physically accurate sky tones:\n//   \u2022 Dawn   \u2014 warm pink-cream (sunrise about to break)\n//   \u2022 Day    \u2014 bright cream (the script's default)\n//   \u2022 Sunset \u2014 saturated orange-red (golden hour pushed past golden)\n//   \u2022 Night  \u2014 dark navy (deep enough that light pigments pop against it)\n//\n// The expensive part of a LERP frame is the full re-render: localized\n// render can't be used because PAPER_*_BASE affects every cell's KM\n// substrate, not just the active rect. At SCALE 1.75 this is a few ms\n// per frame, sustained for ~1.5 seconds during transitions \u2014 fine for\n// an explicit user-driven change.\n\nconst TIME_PRESETS = {\n  dawn:   [0.97, 0.85, 0.78],\n  day:    [0.985, 0.965, 0.92],\n  sunset: [0.85, 0.50, 0.38],\n  night:  [0.12, 0.16, 0.28],\n};\nconst TIME_LERP_FRAMES = 90;     // ~1.5 sec at 60 fps\n\nlet timeLerp = null;             // { fromR/G/B, toR/G/B, frame, total }\n\nfunction startTimeTransition(targetName) {\n  const target = TIME_PRESETS[targetName];\n  if (!target) return;\n  // Snapshot the current PAPER_*_BASE so an interrupting selection\n  // mid-LERP starts smoothly from wherever we are, not from the\n  // previous LERP's origin.\n  timeLerp = {\n    fromR: PAPER_R_BASE, fromG: PAPER_G_BASE, fromB: PAPER_B_BASE,\n    toR: target[0],      toG: target[1],      toB: target[2],\n    frame: 0, total: TIME_LERP_FRAMES,\n  };\n}\n\n// Called from the main rAF loop. Returns true if a transition frame ran\n// \u2014 the loop uses this to force a render even when no painting is active.\nfunction timeStep() {\n  if (!timeLerp) return false;\n  const t = timeLerp.frame / timeLerp.total;\n  // Cubic ease-in-out \u2014 smoother than linear, no JS Math import needed\n  const e = t < 0.5\n    ? 4 * t * t * t\n    : 1 - Math.pow(-2 * t + 2, 3) / 2;\n  PAPER_R_BASE = timeLerp.fromR + (timeLerp.toR - timeLerp.fromR) * e;\n  PAPER_G_BASE = timeLerp.fromG + (timeLerp.toG - timeLerp.fromG) * e;\n  PAPER_B_BASE = timeLerp.fromB + (timeLerp.toB - timeLerp.fromB) * e;\n  // Theme (UI text/panel) follows luminance; body bg follows the paper\n  // color when Transparent is off. Both must be re-applied every frame\n  // so the chrome transitions in sync with the canvas.\n  applyTheme();\n  applyBodyBg();\n  timeLerp.frame++;\n  if (timeLerp.frame > timeLerp.total) {\n    // Final cleanup: rebuild the pigment swatches (which use paper as\n    // KM substrate) and the cursor color preview. These are skipped\n    // during the LERP to keep per-frame cost down \u2014 the user sees them\n    // catch up at the end, which is barely perceptible since they\n    // animate the same color via CSS transitions on top of the JS reset.\n    buildPigmentSwatches();\n    applyCursorColor();\n    timeLerp = null;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nconst timeOfDaySelect = document.getElementById('time-of-day');\ntimeOfDaySelect.addEventListener('change', () => {\n  startTimeTransition(timeOfDaySelect.value);\n});",

    "name": "str_replace",
    "input": {
      "description": "Replace paper-color LERP system with pigment wash system",
      "new_str": "// ============================================================\n// TIME OF DAY \u2014 sky wash layer (v0.12.1)\n// ============================================================\n// Each time preset is a one-shot pigment wash painted across the\n// canvas using actual pigments, layered on top of whatever was there.\n// Unlike v0.12's paper-color LERP, the wash mixes with subsequent\n// painting via KM compositing \u2014 like applying a real watercolor sky\n// wash before painting a foreground.\n//\n// The wash spreads dabs over many frames (~2 sec) so the user watches\n// it apply, and so the bleed sim has time to smooth between dabs\n// instead of all dabs landing simultaneously and stacking into hard\n// edges. Many overlapping low-strength dabs accumulate into a soft\n// continuous wash \u2014 natural wet-on-wet behavior since the canvas\n// starts at wet=0.62.\n//\n// Gradient skies (dawn/sunset) define bands at different y-fractions.\n// Each dab samples the band stochastically based on its y position \u2014\n// near a band boundary, it's a coin flip between the two adjacent\n// pigments. The bleed sim smooths the boundary into a soft transition\n// rather than a hard stripe.\n\nconst TIME_WASH_FRAMES = 120;        // ~2 sec wash application\nconst TIME_WASH_DABS_PER_FRAME = 3;  // dabs / frame, distributed randomly\n\n// Each preset is an array of bands. Each band: { y, pig, str }.\n// y is the canvas-fraction (0=top, 1=bottom). pig is a pigment index\n// or WATER_INDEX. str is the paintAt strength.\n//\n// At any y between bands, dabs are picked stochastically from the two\n// adjacent bands weighted by proximity \u2014 so a dab at the midpoint has\n// 50/50 odds of either pigment. Many random samples across the band\n// give a smooth gradient.\nconst TIME_WASHES = {\n  day: [\n    // Uniform light blue \u2014 gentle, no gradient\n    { y: 0.0, pig: 2, str: 0.10 },\n    { y: 1.0, pig: 2, str: 0.10 },\n  ],\n  night: [\n    // Deeper saturated blue. Mild gradient \u2014 darker at top.\n    { y: 0.0, pig: 2, str: 0.32 },\n    { y: 1.0, pig: 2, str: 0.22 },\n  ],\n  sunset: [\n    // Cool dusk at top, rose mid, warm yellow toward the horizon.\n    // The vertical color shift reads as a sky at golden hour.\n    { y: 0.0, pig: 2, str: 0.14 },   // cerulean top\n    { y: 0.45, pig: 0, str: 0.18 },  // rose mid (a bit above center)\n    { y: 1.0, pig: 1, str: 0.20 },   // yellow horizon\n  ],\n  dawn: [\n    // Same structure as sunset but softer/cooler \u2014 pre-sunrise feel.\n    { y: 0.0, pig: 2, str: 0.09 },\n    { y: 0.5, pig: 0, str: 0.11 },\n    { y: 1.0, pig: 1, str: 0.11 },\n  ],\n};\n\nlet timeWash = null;  // { preset, frame }\n\nfunction startTimeWash(presetName) {\n  // Cancel any in-flight wash and start a new one. Re-selecting a\n  // preset (via Off \u2192 preset \u2192 Off \u2192 preset) lets users layer multiple\n  // washes if they want denser sky tone.\n  const spec = TIME_WASHES[presetName];\n  if (!spec) { timeWash = null; return; }\n  timeWash = { preset: presetName, frame: 0 };\n}\n\n// Pick a pigment + strength for a dab at fractional y. Walks the band\n// list to find the bracket [a, b], then stochastically picks one of\n// the two weighted by proximity to b. At the top/bottom edges the\n// pick collapses to the boundary band (t=0 or t=1).\nfunction washSampleBand(preset, yFrac) {\n  const bands = TIME_WASHES[preset];\n  let i = 0;\n  while (i < bands.length - 1 && bands[i + 1].y < yFrac) i++;\n  if (i >= bands.length - 1) return bands[bands.length - 1];\n  const a = bands[i], b = bands[i + 1];\n  const t = (yFrac - a.y) / (b.y - a.y);\n  return Math.random() < t ? b : a;\n}\n\n// Called every frame from the main loop. Returns true if the wash\n// painted this frame (for the render gate, though normal paintAt\n// already wakes the canvas so this is mostly informational).\nfunction timeWashStep() {\n  if (!timeWash) return false;\n  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n  const pxToGridRadius = (GW / rect.width) * 0.5;\n  for (let i = 0; i < TIME_WASH_DABS_PER_FRAME; i++) {\n    // Uniform random across the whole canvas. Over the wash's ~360\n    // total dabs at ~70-150 px brush size, this gives multiple-coverage\n    // of every region, smoothing the wash into a continuous tone.\n    const x = Math.random() * GW;\n    const y = Math.random() * GH;\n    const yFrac = y / GH;\n    const band = washSampleBand(timeWash.preset, yFrac);\n    const brushPx = 70 + Math.random() * 80;\n    paintAt(x, y, brushPx * pxToGridRadius, band.pig, band.str);\n  }\n  timeWash.frame++;\n  if (timeWash.frame >= TIME_WASH_FRAMES) timeWash = null;\n  return true;\n}\n\nconst timeOfDaySelect = document.getElementById('time-of-day');\ntimeOfDaySelect.addEventListener('change', () => {\n  startTimeWash(timeOfDaySelect.value);\n});",
      "old_str": "// ============================================================\n// TIME OF DAY \u2014 paper color animation layer (v0.12)\n// ============================================================\n// Independent from the weather animation: each time preset has a target\n// paper color (PAPER_R/G/B_BASE), and selecting one starts a smooth LERP\n// from the current substrate to that target. The LERP runs for\n// TIME_LERP_FRAMES, applying theme + body-bg every frame and forcing a\n// full render so the canvas reflects the new color in real time.\n//\n// Targets are visual moods, not physically accurate sky tones:\n//   \u2022 Dawn   \u2014 warm pink-cream (sunrise about to break)\n//   \u2022 Day    \u2014 bright cream (the script's default)\n//   \u2022 Sunset \u2014 saturated orange-red (golden hour pushed past golden)\n//   \u2022 Night  \u2014 dark navy (deep enough that light pigments pop against it)\n//\n// The expensive part of a LERP frame is the full re-render: localized\n// render can't be used because PAPER_*_BASE affects every cell's KM\n// substrate, not just the active rect. At SCALE 1.75 this is a few ms\n// per frame, sustained for ~1.5 seconds during transitions \u2014 fine for\n// an explicit user-driven change.\n\nconst TIME_PRESETS = {\n  dawn:   [0.97, 0.85, 0.78],\n  day:    [0.985, 0.965, 0.92],\n  sunset: [0.85, 0.50, 0.38],\n  night:  [0.12, 0.16, 0.28],\n};\nconst TIME_LERP_FRAMES = 90;     // ~1.5 sec at 60 fps\n\nlet timeLerp = null;             // { fromR/G/B, toR/G/B, frame, total }\n\nfunction startTimeTransition(targetName) {\n  const target = TIME_PRESETS[targetName];\n  if (!target) return;\n  // Snapshot the current PAPER_*_BASE so an interrupting selection\n  // mid-LERP starts smoothly from wherever we are, not from the\n  // previous LERP's origin.\n  timeLerp = {\n    fromR: PAPER_R_BASE, fromG: PAPER_G_BASE, fromB: PAPER_B_BASE,\n    toR: target[0],      toG: target[1],      toB: target[2],\n    frame: 0, total: TIME_LERP_FRAMES,\n  };\n}\n\n// Called from the main rAF loop. Returns true if a transition frame ran\n// \u2014 the loop uses this to force a render even when no painting is active.\nfunction timeStep() {\n  if (!timeLerp) return false;\n  const t = timeLerp.frame / timeLerp.total;\n  // Cubic ease-in-out \u2014 smoother than linear, no JS Math import needed\n  const e = t < 0.5\n    ? 4 * t * t * t\n    : 1 - Math.pow(-2 * t + 2, 3) / 2;\n  PAPER_R_BASE = timeLerp.fromR + (timeLerp.toR - timeLerp.fromR) * e;\n  PAPER_G_BASE = timeLerp.fromG + (timeLerp.toG - timeLerp.fromG) * e;\n  PAPER_B_BASE = timeLerp.fromB + (timeLerp.toB - timeLerp.fromB) * e;\n  // Theme (UI text/panel) follows luminance; body bg follows the paper\n  // color when Transparent is off. Both must be re-applied every frame\n  // so the chrome transitions in sync with the canvas.\n  applyTheme();\n  applyBodyBg();\n  timeLerp.frame++;\n  if (timeLerp.frame > timeLerp.total) {\n    // Final cleanup: rebuild the pigment swatches (which use paper as\n    // KM substrate) and the cursor color preview. These are skipped\n    // during the LERP to keep per-frame cost down \u2014 the user sees them\n    // catch up at the end, which is barely perceptible since they\n    // animate the same color via CSS transitions on top of the JS reset.\n    buildPigmentSwatches();\n    applyCursorColor();\n    timeLerp = null;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nconst timeOfDaySelect = document.getElementById('time-of-day');\ntimeOfDaySelect.addEventListener('change', () => {\n  startTimeTransition(timeOfDaySelect.value);\n});",

Selecting `Sunset + Thunderstorm` paints an orange sky and the cerulean rain hits naturally over it; lightning bolts in yellow pop against the dusky background.       "new_str": "// ============================================================\n// TIME OF DAY \u2014 sky wash layer (v0.12.2)\n// ============================================================\n// Each time preset is a one-shot pigment wash painted across the\n// canvas using actual pigments, layered on top of whatever was there.\n// The wash mixes with subsequent painting via KM compositing \u2014 like\n// applying a real watercolor sky wash before painting a foreground.\n//\n// Preset structure (v0.12.2): each preset is a list of independent\n// pigment \"layers\". Each layer has a single pigment and a y\u2192strength\n// curve. A dab at fractional position yFrac evaluates EVERY layer's\n// curve at yFrac and emits a paintAt for each non-zero contribution,\n// all at the same (x, y) and brush size. This lets pigments stack\n// on top of each other (e.g. cerulean + rose at the top of night for\n// a deeper purple-blue) while still supporting vertical gradients\n// (sunset's cerulean\u2192rose\u2192yellow falls out of three curves with\n// non-overlapping non-zero regions in the middle, overlapping at the\n// transition zones).\n//\n// The wash spreads dabs over ~0.75 sec so the user watches it apply,\n// and so the bleed sim has frame-spacing to smooth between dabs\n// instead of all dabs landing simultaneously. Many overlapping\n// low-strength dabs accumulate into a soft continuous tone \u2014 natural\n// wet-on-wet behavior since the canvas starts at wet=0.62.\n\nconst TIME_WASH_FRAMES = 45;          // ~0.75 sec at 60 fps\nconst TIME_WASH_DABS_PER_FRAME = 8;   // total \u2248 360 dab positions per wash\n\n// Each preset is an array of layers. Each layer is { pig, curve }\n// where curve is an array of [yFrac, strength] control points. The\n// curve is piecewise-linear; values of yFrac outside the curve's\n// range return 0 strength (so the layer doesn't contribute outside\n// its declared region).\nconst TIME_WASHES = {\n  day: [\n    // Light uniform cerulean \u2014 gentle sky tint.\n    { pig: 2, curve: [[0.0, 0.08], [1.0, 0.08]] },\n  ],\n  night: [\n    // Cerulean base + rose darkening layer. The two pigments KM-mix\n    // into deep blue-purple. Rose is stronger at top (zenith reads\n    // darker than horizon), tapering toward the bottom.\n    { pig: 2, curve: [[0.0, 0.26], [1.0, 0.20]] },   // cerulean\n    { pig: 0, curve: [[0.0, 0.18], [1.0, 0.10]] },   // rose, top-heavy\n  ],\n  sunset: [\n    // Three-layer gradient: dusk-blue zenith \u2192 rose mid \u2192 yellow\n    // horizon. Each layer's non-zero region overlaps its neighbor\n    // at the transition zone so the bleed sim cross-fades cleanly.\n    { pig: 2, curve: [[0.0, 0.13], [0.45, 0.0]] },              // cerulean fading out\n    { pig: 0, curve: [[0.0, 0.0], [0.45, 0.16], [1.0, 0.0]] },  // rose peaks at 0.45\n    { pig: 1, curve: [[0.45, 0.0], [1.0, 0.20]] },              // yellow rising to horizon\n  ],\n  dawn: [\n    // Softer cousin of sunset \u2014 same shape, lower strengths, peak\n    // pushed slightly toward the top (sky still cool, not yet warm).\n    { pig: 2, curve: [[0.0, 0.09], [0.55, 0.0]] },\n    { pig: 0, curve: [[0.0, 0.0], [0.55, 0.11], [1.0, 0.0]] },\n    { pig: 1, curve: [[0.55, 0.0], [1.0, 0.11]] },\n  ],\n};\n\nlet timeWash = null;  // { preset, frame }\n\nfunction startTimeWash(presetName) {\n  // Cancel any in-flight wash and start a new one. Switching presets\n  // (via Day \u2192 Night, etc.) interrupts mid-application \u2014 the partially-\n  // applied first wash stays on the canvas, and the new wash layers\n  // on top of it from a fresh start.\n  const spec = TIME_WASHES[presetName];\n  if (!spec) { timeWash = null; return; }\n  timeWash = { preset: presetName, frame: 0 };\n}\n\n// Piecewise-linear interpolation along a single layer's curve. yFrac\n// outside the curve's declared range returns 0 \u2014 the layer is absent\n// from that region of the canvas.\nfunction evalCurve(curve, yFrac) {\n  if (yFrac < curve[0][0] || yFrac > curve[curve.length - 1][0]) return 0;\n  for (let i = 0; i < curve.length - 1; i++) {\n    const y0 = curve[i][0], s0 = curve[i][1];\n    const y1 = curve[i + 1][0], s1 = curve[i + 1][1];\n    if (yFrac >= y0 && yFrac <= y1) {\n      const t = (yFrac - y0) / (y1 - y0);\n      return s0 + (s1 - s0) * t;\n    }\n  }\n  return 0;\n}\n\n// Called every frame from the main loop. Returns true if the wash\n// painted this frame (informational \u2014 paintAt already wakes the sim).\nfunction timeWashStep() {\n  if (!timeWash) return false;\n  const layers = TIME_WASHES[timeWash.preset];\n  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n  const pxToGridRadius = (GW / rect.width) * 0.5;\n  for (let i = 0; i < TIME_WASH_DABS_PER_FRAME; i++) {\n    const x = Math.random() * GW;\n    const y = Math.random() * GH;\n    const yFrac = y / GH;\n    const brushPx = 70 + Math.random() * 80;\n    const gridRadius = brushPx * pxToGridRadius;\n    // Evaluate every layer's curve at this y. Each non-zero result\n    // produces a paintAt at the same (x, y) \u2014 pigments stack into the\n    // same cells, KM compositing handles the visual mix.\n    for (let L = 0; L < layers.length; L++) {\n      const layer = layers[L];\n      const str = evalCurve(layer.curve, yFrac);\n      if (str > 0.001) paintAt(x, y, gridRadius, layer.pig, str);\n    }\n  }\n  timeWash.frame++;\n  if (timeWash.frame >= TIME_WASH_FRAMES) timeWash = null;\n  return true;\n}\n\nconst timeOfDaySelect = document.getElementById('time-of-day');\ntimeOfDaySelect.addEventListener('change', () => {\n  startTimeWash(timeOfDaySelect.value);\n});",
      "old_str": "// ============================================================\n// TIME OF DAY \u2014 sky wash layer (v0.12.1)\n// ============================================================\n// Each time preset is a one-shot pigment wash painted across the\n// canvas using actual pigments, layered on top of whatever was there.\n// Unlike v0.12's paper-color LERP, the wash mixes with subsequent\n// painting via KM compositing \u2014 like applying a real watercolor sky\n// wash before painting a foreground.\n//\n// The wash spreads dabs over many frames (~2 sec) so the user watches\n// it apply, and so the bleed sim has time to smooth between dabs\n// instead of all dabs landing simultaneously and stacking into hard\n// edges. Many overlapping low-strength dabs accumulate into a soft\n// continuous wash \u2014 natural wet-on-wet behavior since the canvas\n// starts at wet=0.62.\n//\n// Gradient skies (dawn/sunset) define bands at different y-fractions.\n// Each dab samples the band stochastically based on its y position \u2014\n// near a band boundary, it's a coin flip between the two adjacent\n// pigments. The bleed sim smooths the boundary into a soft transition\n// rather than a hard stripe.\n\nconst TIME_WASH_FRAMES = 120;        // ~2 sec wash application\nconst TIME_WASH_DABS_PER_FRAME = 3;  // dabs / frame, distributed randomly\n\n// Each preset is an array of bands. Each band: { y, pig, str }.\n// y is the canvas-fraction (0=top, 1=bottom). pig is a pigment index\n// or WATER_INDEX. str is the paintAt strength.\n//\n// At any y between bands, dabs are picked stochastically from the two\n// adjacent bands weighted by proximity \u2014 so a dab at the midpoint has\n// 50/50 odds of either pigment. Many random samples across the band\n// give a smooth gradient.\nconst TIME_WASHES = {\n  day: [\n    // Uniform light blue \u2014 gentle, no gradient\n    { y: 0.0, pig: 2, str: 0.10 },\n    { y: 1.0, pig: 2, str: 0.10 },\n  ],\n  night: [\n    // Deeper saturated blue. Mild gradient \u2014 darker at top.\n    { y: 0.0, pig: 2, str: 0.32 },\n    { y: 1.0, pig: 2, str: 0.22 },\n  ],\n  sunset: [\n    // Cool dusk at top, rose mid, warm yellow toward the horizon.\n    // The vertical color shift reads as a sky at golden hour.\n    { y: 0.0, pig: 2, str: 0.14 },   // cerulean top\n    { y: 0.45, pig: 0, str: 0.18 },  // rose mid (a bit above center)\n    { y: 1.0, pig: 1, str: 0.20 },   // yellow horizon\n  ],\n  dawn: [\n    // Same structure as sunset but softer/cooler \u2014 pre-sunrise feel.\n    { y: 0.0, pig: 2, str: 0.09 },\n    { y: 0.5, pig: 0, str: 0.11 },\n    { y: 1.0, pig: 1, str: 0.11 },\n  ],\n};\n\nlet timeWash = null;  // { preset, frame }\n\nfunction startTimeWash(presetName) {\n  // Cancel any in-flight wash and start a new one. Re-selecting a\n  // preset (via Off \u2192 preset \u2192 Off \u2192 preset) lets users layer multiple\n  // washes if they want denser sky tone.\n  const spec = TIME_WASHES[presetName];\n  if (!spec) { timeWash = null; return; }\n  timeWash = { preset: presetName, frame: 0 };\n}\n\n// Pick a pigment + strength for a dab at fractional y. Walks the band\n// list to find the bracket [a, b], then stochastically picks one of\n// the two weighted by proximity to b. At the top/bottom edges the\n// pick collapses to the boundary band (t=0 or t=1).\nfunction washSampleBand(preset, yFrac) {\n  const bands = TIME_WASHES[preset];\n  let i = 0;\n  while (i < bands.length - 1 && bands[i + 1].y < yFrac) i++;\n  if (i >= bands.length - 1) return bands[bands.length - 1];\n  const a = bands[i], b = bands[i + 1];\n  const t = (yFrac - a.y) / (b.y - a.y);\n  return Math.random() < t ? b : a;\n}\n\n// Called every frame from the main loop. Returns true if the wash\n// painted this frame (for the render gate, though normal paintAt\n// already wakes the canvas so this is mostly informational).\nfunction timeWashStep() {\n  if (!timeWash) return false;\n  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n  const pxToGridRadius = (GW / rect.width) * 0.5;\n  for (let i = 0; i < TIME_WASH_DABS_PER_FRAME; i++) {\n    // Uniform random across the whole canvas. Over the wash's ~360\n    // total dabs at ~70-150 px brush size, this gives multiple-coverage\n    // of every region, smoothing the wash into a continuous tone.\n    const x = Math.random() * GW;\n    const y = Math.random() * GH;\n    const yFrac = y / GH;\n    const band = washSampleBand(timeWash.preset, yFrac);\n    const brushPx = 70 + Math.random() * 80;\n    paintAt(x, y, brushPx * pxToGridRadius, band.pig, band.str);\n  }\n  timeWash.frame++;\n  if (timeWash.frame >= TIME_WASH_FRAMES) timeWash = null;\n  return true;\n}\n\nconst timeOfDaySelect = document.getElementById('time-of-day');\ntimeOfDaySelect.addEventListener('change', () => {\n  startTimeWash(timeOfDaySelect.value);\n});",

Below the 1-second ceiling even on slightly-throttled framerates.\n\n**Layered structure.** Each preset is now a list of independent pigment **layers**, each with its own y\u2192strength curve. A dab at fractional y evaluates *every* layer's curve and emits a `paintAt` for each non-zero contribution at the same `(x, y)` and brush size. This lets pigments **stack into the same cells** so KM compositing handles the visual mix, rather than picking one pigment stochastically per dab.\n\n**Night, before:** cerulean only, 0.32 \u2192 0.22 top to bottom.\n**Night, now:** cerulean (0.26 \u2192 0.20) **+** rose (0.18 \u2192 0.10) stacked. Rose darkens cerulean toward deep blue-purple via KM mixing. Both layers are top-heavy so the zenith reads darker than the horizon \u2014 like an actual night sky.\n\n**Cross-fades (sunset/dawn) are now true overlaps** instead of stochastic choices:\n- At y=0.225 in sunset: cerulean=0.065 (fading) **and** rose=0.080 (rising) deposit at the same cell \u2192 KM mixes them into dusky purple.\n- At y=0.725: rose=0.080 (fading) **and** yellow=0.100 (rising) \u2192 orange.\n- Transitions are physically blended, not random sampling, so the gradient is smoother.",

      "description": "Replace snowing with two-phase wash+lift version",
      "new_str": "// ----- SNOWING preset (v0.12.3 \u2014 wash + lift) -------------------------\n// Two-phase preset:\n//   Phase 1 (first ~0.5 sec): cerulean wash painted across the canvas,\n//     same dab pattern as the time-of-day washes. Builds a blue sky\n//     for the snow to fall against.\n//   Phase 2 (after wash, ongoing): water-brush drops fall and flutter,\n//     each one LIFTING the deposited cerulean back into suspension.\n//     The cells where flakes land become less dense in pigment \u2014 paper\n//     color shows through more, reading as white-ish snow trails.\n//\n// The water brush also adds wet + pressure, which keeps the canvas\n// saturated and lets the lifted pigment bleed/spread naturally. The\n// granulation coefficient of cerulean (\u03b3=0.75) makes the residual\n// pigment settle into paper crevices, giving the white trails a soft\n// textured edge instead of a hard line.\n//\n// 90% of drops are pure water (lift), 10% are small cerulean dabs at\n// low strength \u2014 these refresh the sky as flakes deplete it, keeping\n// the contrast alive over long sessions.\n\nconst SNOW_WASH_FRAMES = 30;            // ~0.5 sec wash before flakes start\nconst SNOW_WASH_DABS_PER_FRAME = 8;\nconst SNOW_WASH_STRENGTH = 0.20;        // moderate blue, dense enough to lift visibly\nconst SNOW_MAX_DROPS = 16;\nconst SNOW_SPAWN_PER_SEC = 30;\n\n// State held in a single object so a mode switch can clear it via\n// snowState = null. lazy-init on the first step after mode start.\nlet snowState = null;\n\nfunction snowingInit() {\n  snowState = { washFrame: 0, drops: [] };\n}\n\nfunction snowSpawnFlake() {\n  return {\n    x: Math.random() * GW,\n    y: -10 - Math.random() * 30,\n    vy: 0.9 + Math.random() * 1.3,\n    flutterPhase: Math.random() * Math.PI * 2,\n    flutterAmp: 0.3 + Math.random() * 0.5,\n    flutterFreq: 0.04 + Math.random() * 0.04,\n    brushPx: 6 + Math.random() * 10,\n    // 90% water (lifts cerulean \u2192 reveals paper-cream \"white\"),\n    // 10% tiny cerulean replenish dabs so the sky doesn't deplete.\n    pigment: Math.random() < 0.90 ? WATER_INDEX : 2,\n    strength: 0.10 + Math.random() * 0.10,    // only used by cerulean dabs\n  };\n}\n\nfunction snowingStep() {\n  if (!snowState) snowingInit();\n  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n  const pxToGridRadius = (GW / rect.width) * 0.5;\n\n  // Phase 1: blue wash. Pure-cerulean dabs scattered across the canvas\n  // at uniform strength \u2014 a flat blue sky. Reuses the same dab pattern\n  // and tuning as the time-of-day day preset.\n  if (snowState.washFrame < SNOW_WASH_FRAMES) {\n    for (let i = 0; i < SNOW_WASH_DABS_PER_FRAME; i++) {\n      const x = Math.random() * GW;\n      const y = Math.random() * GH;\n      const brushPx = 70 + Math.random() * 80;\n      paintAt(x, y, brushPx * pxToGridRadius, 2, SNOW_WASH_STRENGTH);\n    }\n    snowState.washFrame++;\n    return;  // hold off spawning flakes until wash is laid down\n  }\n\n  // Phase 2: flakes spawn and fall. Same spawn/step/cull shape as the\n  // other drop-based presets, but the per-drop pigment is mostly the\n  // water brush \u2014 the visible effect on the canvas is paper showing\n  // through where flakes pass, not pigment being added.\n  const spawnProb = SNOW_SPAWN_PER_SEC / 60;\n  while (snowState.drops.length < SNOW_MAX_DROPS && Math.random() < spawnProb) {\n    snowState.drops.push(snowSpawnFlake());\n  }\n  for (let i = snowState.drops.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {\n    const d = snowState.drops[i];\n    d.flutterPhase += d.flutterFreq;\n    d.x += Math.sin(d.flutterPhase) * d.flutterAmp;\n    d.y += d.vy;\n    if (d.y > GH + 10 || d.x < -10 || d.x > GW + 10) { snowState.drops.splice(i, 1); continue; }\n    if (d.y >= 0 && d.y < GH) {\n      paintAt(d.x, d.y, d.brushPx * pxToGridRadius, d.pigment, d.strength);\n    }\n  }\n}",

Water flakes fall and flutter (sinusoidal x-drift) and *lift* the deposited cerulean \u2014 pigment goes from `d[]` back to `g[]`, then bleeds outward, leaving thinner blue where the flake hit. Paper color shows through more in those spots, reading as white-ish snow trails. The 10% cerulean dabs replenish the sky slowly so it doesn't deplete over long sessions.\n\n**Infocons**\n\nEvery toggle, button, and dropdown now has a `\u24d8` marker next to its label and a `data-tip` attribute. Hovering anywhere on the control shows a custom tooltip above it (themed to match the panel, multi-line, max-width 240px). The marker is subtle by default (opacity 0.55), brightens on hover. Pointer-events disabled on the marker itself so it doesn't interfere with click targets.\n\nTooltips cover what each control does plus a short hint about *why*, e.g. **Edge darkening**: \"Concentrates pigment at the edges of wet regions \u2014 the way real watercolor pools at boundaries when the water dries.\" **Re-wet**: \"Restores wetness across the whole canvas and lifts 30% of deposited pigment back into suspension so dried strokes become mobile again.\"",

Works well\n// layered on top of a sunset or dawn wash where the rose flecks pick\n// up the warm tones.\n\nconst SNOW_ADD_MAX_DROPS = 16;\nconst SNOW_ADD_SPAWN_PER_SEC = 24;\n\nlet snowAddDrops = [];\n\nfunction snowAddSpawnDrop() {\n  return {\n    x: Math.random() * GW,\n    y: -10 - Math.random() * 30,\n    vy: 0.9 + Math.random() * 1.3,\n    flutterPhase: Math.random() * Math.PI * 2,\n    flutterAmp: 0.3 + Math.random() * 0.4,\n    flutterFreq: 0.04 + Math.random() * 0.04,\n    brushPx: 4 + Math.random() * 8,\n    // Cerulean dominates with occasional rose for warm flecks; \u03b3=0.75\n    // granulation makes the dabs settle into a snowflake-like speckle.\n    pigment: Math.random() < 0.88 ? 2 : 0,\n    strength: 0.10 + Math.random() * 0.10,\n  };\n}\n\nfunction snowingAdditiveStep() {\n  const spawnProb = SNOW_ADD_SPAWN_PER_SEC / 60;\n  while (snowAddDrops.length < SNOW_ADD_MAX_DROPS && Math.random() < spawnProb) {\n    snowAddDrops.push(snowAddSpawnDrop());\n  }\n  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n  const pxToGridRadius = (GW / rect.width) * 0.5;\n  for (let i = snowAddDrops.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {\n    const d = snowAddDrops[i];\n    d.flutterPhase += d.flutterFreq;\n    d.x += Math.sin(d.flutterPhase) * d.flutterAmp;\n    d.y += d.vy;\n    if (d.y > GH + 10 || d.x < -10 || d.x > GW + 10) { snowAddDrops.splice(i, 1); continue; }\n    if (d.y >= 0 && d.y < GH) {\n      paintAt(d.x, d.y, d.brushPx * pxToGridRadius, d.pigment, d.strength);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n// ----- dispatcher -------------------------------------------------------\nfunction animationStep() {\n  switch (animationMode) {\n    case 'ai':                aiStep();                break;\n    case 'rainy':             rainStep();              break;\n    case 'sunny':             sunnyStep();             break;\n    case 'windy':             windyStep();             break;\n    case 'thunderstorm':      thunderstormStep();      break;\n    case 'partlyCloudy':      cloudsStep();            break;\n    case 'snowing':           snowingStep();           break;\n    case 'snowingAdditive':   snowingAdditiveStep();   break;\n    // 'off' falls through to no-op\n  }\n}",
      "old_str": "    d.x += Math.sin(d.flutterPhase) * d.flutterAmp;\n    d.y += d.vy;\n    if (d.y > GH + 10 || d.x < -10 || d.x > GW + 10) { snowState.drops.splice(i, 1); continue; }\n    if (d.y >= 0 && d.y < GH) {\n      paintAt(d.x, d.y, d.brushPx * pxToGridRadius, d.pigment, d.strength);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n// ----- dispatcher -------------------------------------------------------\nfunction animationStep() {\n  switch (animationMode) {\n    case 'ai':           aiStep();           break;\n    case 'rainy':        rainStep();         break;\n    case 'sunny':        sunnyStep();        break;\n    case 'windy':        windyStep();        break;\n    case 'thunderstorm': thunderstormStep(); break;\n    case 'partlyCloudy': cloudsStep();       break;\n    case 'snowing':      snowingStep();      break;\n    // 'off' falls through to no-op\n  }\n}",
